questions.q: The thoracic duct, the largest lymphatic vessel, drains lymph from:
options: A. Only the right side of the body above the diaphragm
B. The entire body except the right upper quadrant (right arm, right chest, right head/neck)
C. The entire body equally on both sides
D. Only the lower extremities and abdomen
E. The right arm and right thorax only

questions.q: The right lymphatic duct drains lymph from:
options: A. Both lower limbs and the abdomen
B. The abdomen and thorax only
C. The right upper quadrant (right arm, right thorax, right head and neck)
D. The left side of the body
E. The entire thoracic cavity

questions.q: The cisterna chyli is:
options: A. The main duct collecting lymph from the right arm
B. A large lymphatic sac at the base of the thoracic duct that collects lymph from the intestinal and lumbar trunks
C. A lymph node in the mesentery
D. The valve at the junction of the thoracic duct and subclavian vein
E. A lymphatic capillary plexus within the intestinal villi

questions.q: In a lymph node, B lymphocytes are concentrated in the:
options: A. Medullary sinuses
B. Paracortex (deep cortex)
C. Follicles of the superficial cortex
D. Efferent lymphatic vessels near the hilum
E. Trabeculae

questions.q: The paracortex (deep cortex) of a lymph node is primarily populated by:
options: A. B cells organized in germinal centers
B. Macrophages and neutrophils
C. T lymphocytes (the T cell zone)
D. Plasma cells actively secreting antibodies
E. NK cells

questions.q: The thymus is a primary lymphoid organ because:
options: A. It filters blood for antigens like the spleen
B. It is the site where T lymphocytes mature and undergo positive and negative selection
C. It produces B cells from hematopoietic stem cells
D. It stores mature lymphocytes for rapid deployment
E. It produces hematopoietic stem cells directly

questions.q: The tonsils and Peyer's patches are classified as:
options: A. Primary lymphoid organs (like thymus and bone marrow)
B. Encapsulated secondary lymphoid organs (like lymph nodes)
C. Mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT)
D. Lymphatic trunks
E. Red pulp structures of the spleen

questions.q: Peyer's patches are aggregations of lymphoid tissue found in the:
options: A. Liver sinusoids
B. Red pulp of the spleen
C. Lamina propria and submucosa of the ileum (small intestine)
D. Appendix only
E. Lung parenchyma

questions.q: The white pulp of the spleen consists of:
options: A. Sinusoids filled with blood for filtration
B. Lymphoid tissue (lymphocytes surrounding central arterioles) for immune responses
C. Erythrocytes being stored as a blood reservoir
D. Phagocytic macrophages primarily engulfing old RBCs
E. Plasma cells actively secreting immunoglobulins only

questions.q: The red pulp of the spleen primarily functions to:
options: A. Produce T lymphocytes and conduct selection
B. Filter lymph fluid from the lymphatic vessels
C. Remove old, damaged, or abnormal erythrocytes from circulation and serve as a blood reservoir
D. Produce antibodies against blood-borne antigens
E. Serve as the primary site for B cell maturation

questions.q: Primary lymphoid organs, where lymphocytes are produced and mature, are the:
options: A. Lymph nodes and spleen
B. Tonsils and Peyer's patches
C. Red bone marrow and thymus
D. Spleen and thymus only
E. Lymph nodes and red bone marrow

questions.q: Secondary lymphoid organs, where lymphocytes encounter antigens and initiate immune responses, include:
options: A. Red bone marrow and thymus only
B. Lymph nodes, spleen, tonsils, and MALT
C. Only the spleen and red bone marrow
D. Thymus and lymph nodes only
E. Bone marrow, lymph nodes, and thymus combined

questions.q: Germinal centers within lymphoid follicles are sites of:
options: A. T cell activation and proliferation
B. Macrophage phagocytosis of debris
C. Rapid B cell proliferation, somatic hypermutation, and differentiation into plasma cells and memory B cells in response to antigen
D. NK cell activation and cytotoxic activity
E. Hematopoiesis (blood cell production)

questions.q: The intestinal lymphatic trunk, which collects fat-laden lymph (chyle) from the small intestine lacteals, is the:
options: A. Lumbar trunk
B. Jugular trunk
C. Intestinal trunk
D. Bronchomediastinal trunk
E. Subclavian trunk

questions.q: Compared to blood capillaries, lymphatic capillaries are:
options: A. Lined by thick stratified epithelium
B. Blind-ended (closed at one end), begin interstitially, and are more permeable to large molecules, proteins, and cells
C. Connected to the arterial system at their origin
D. Impermeable to all proteins over 10 kDa
E. Found only in the thoracic and abdominal cavities

questions.q: In addition to immune functions, the spleen also serves as a reservoir for:
options: A. Lymphocytes only
B. Platelets and a reserve volume of blood (including erythrocytes)
C. Plasma cells secreting IgA
D. Dietary iron from absorbed nutrients
E. Vitamin B12 for erythropoiesis

questions.q: Lymphedema (tissue swelling) results from:
options: A. Excessive lymphocyte production overwhelming the lymph nodes
B. Blockage or removal of lymphatic drainage causing fluid accumulation in the interstitium
C. Inflammation of lymph nodes (lymphadenitis) alone
D. Genetic absence of lymphatic vessels
E. Low albumin causing reduced plasma oncotic pressure

questions.q: MALT (mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue) provides immune surveillance specifically at:
options: A. Deep body compartments protected by bone
B. Mucosal surfaces in contact with the external environment (digestive, respiratory, urinary, and reproductive tracts)
C. Central lymphoid organs (thymus and bone marrow) only
D. Bone marrow only
E. The bloodstream as circulating lymphoid tissue

questions.q: Innate immunity differs from adaptive immunity in that innate immunity:
options: A. Requires prior exposure to an antigen to be effective
B. Is mediated specifically by B and T lymphocytes
C. Is non-specific, acts immediately without prior exposure, and has no immunological memory
D. Has specific immunological memory allowing faster responses on re-exposure
E. Produces antigen-specific antibodies

questions.q: The lymph node hilum is:
options: A. The outer capsule surrounding the node
B. Where efferent lymphatic vessels, blood vessels, and nerves enter/exit the node
C. The site of B cell follicles in the cortex
D. The medullary sinus region
E. Where afferent lymphatic vessels enter the node
